If you use [Pixi](https://pixi.sh/), you can create a new isolated environment
with the deployed conda package:

```{r}
#| label: installation-pixi
#| results: asis
glue(
  "
  ```bash
  pixi init -c {org_nm} -c conda-forge ./tidy_env
  cd ./tidy_env
  pixi add {conda$pkg}=={ver}
  ",
  "```",
  "

  Then you can create a task to run the `{pkg_nm}.R` CLI script:

  ```bash
  pixi task add {pkg_nm} \"{pkg_nm}.R\"
  pixi run {pkg_nm} --help
  ",
  "```",
  "

  Or activate the environment and use {pkg_nm} directly in an R environment:

  ```bash
  pixi shell
  R
  ",
  "```",
  "

  ```r
  library({pkg_nm})
  ",
  "```"
)
```
